title: Knee surgery guide and method
abstract: Method and apparatus for performing an anterior distal femoral resection. A distal femoral resection is formed and used as a surface from which to base an anterior distal femoral resection cutting guide apparatus. The cutting guide apparatus may include a position block and an attached cutting guide. The position block is placed against the anterior distal femoral resection, and the orientation of the anterior cutting guide is set, at least in part, based on the anterior distal femoral resection.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of performing an anterior distal femoral resection on a femur using a cutting guide apparatus, the method comprising: performing a proximal tibial resection to form a proximal tibial resection surface; performing an extramedullar distal femoral resection to form a distal femoral resection surface parallel to the proximal tibial resection surface, the distal femoral resection being performed by using a spacer block that is placed against the proximal tibial resection surface; placing a position block against the distal femoral resection surface; and performing a distal femoral anterior resection using an anterior cutting guide that is attached to the position block. 2. A method as in claim 1, further comprising adjusting an anterior/posterior height of the cutting guide relative to the position block prior to performing the distal femoral anterior resection. 3. A method as in claim 1 , further comprising adjusting a rotation of the cutting guide about a longitudinal anatomic axis of the femur or an axis parallel to the longitudinal anatomic axis of the femur prior to performing the distal femoral anterior resection. 4. A method as in claim 1, further comprising attaching the anterior cutting guide to the position block. 5. A method as in claim 1, wherein the distal femoral resection surface is perpendicular to a l ongi tudinal an atomic axis of the femur. 6. A method as in claim 1, wherein placing the position block against the distal femoral resection surface comprises placing the position block against no more than one condyle of the distal femoral resection surface. 7. A method as in claim 1, wherein the act of placing the position block against the distal femoral resection surface is performed prior to performing a posterior chamfer cut. 8. A method as in claim 1 , wherein the act of placing the position block against the distal femoral resection surface is performed after performing a posterior chamfer cut. 9. A method as in claim 1 , wherein the position block comprises a posterior chamfer cutting block. 10. A method as in claim 1, further comprising securing the position block to the distal femoral resection surface using position holes used for a posterior chamfer cutting block. 1 1.
